_comment: Written by scripts/regen-src-golden.mjs (#91). `sha256` must match tests/fixtures/src-bundle.golden.js — tests/src-bundle-parity.test.ts enforces it, so a hand-edited golden, or one regenerated by any means that skips the reason prompt, fails the gate.
reason: #124: add save-backup.ts — the "Backup & Portal" button. Measured in real Chrome: an <a download> click emits NO load/error/abort/loadend event and there is no download API on window, so "portal only after the download is confirmed" (what the issue asked for) is unimplementable — a page cannot know a file reached the disk. The guarantee is therefore inverted: the localStorage backup is the mechanism (synchronous, uncancellable, VERIFIED BY READBACK) and a failed backup CANCELS THE PORTAL; the file download is best-effort and nothing gates on it. Auto-portal gets the verified backup unconditionally but never a silent download (no user gesture trips Chrome automatic-downloads permission, which then fails silently forever). Ring of 3, with a Download Last Backup button so the ring is not write-only.
